channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: England’s Wrestling Church seeks converts with baptisms and body slams
item.description: SHIPLEY, England (AP) — Sitting around a wrestling ring, churchgoers roared as local hero Billy O’Keeffe body-slammed a fighter named Disciple. Beneath stained-glass windows, they whooped and cheered as burly, tattooed wresters tumbled into the aisle during a six-man tag-team battle.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As stock market continues plummeting over tariffs, Trump spends the day at his golf course
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Two days after sending the economy reeling by announcing widespread tariffs, President Donald Trump insisted his trade policies will never change as he remained ensconced in a bubble of wealth and power in Florida.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, CHRIS MEGERIAN and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: India's parliament passes bill that would change Muslim land endowments
item.description: India's parliament passes a bill amending Muslim land endowments, sparking protests over concerns it undermines minority rights and could lead to property confiscation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Officials say a Russian strike in central Ukraine kills 14 people, including six children
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian ballistic missile strike Friday on a central Ukrainian city killed at least 14 people, including six children, Ukrainian officials said, as U.S. and European leaders pressed Russia to accept a ceasefire in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Panamanian ex-leader Martinelli remains in the Nicaraguan Embassy after window to leave expired
item.description: Panama’s ex-President Ricardo Martinelli remains inside the Nicaraguan Embassy more than a year after he first entered and a week after Panama said it would allow him to leave.
item.dc:creator: Alma Solís, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kurdish fighters leave northern city in Syria as part of deal with central government
item.description: ALEPPO, Syria (AP) — Scores of U.S.-backed Kurdish fighters left two neighborhoods in Syria’s northern city of Aleppo Friday as part of a deal with the central government in Damascus, which is expanding its authority in the country.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Experts warn Iran’s nuclear double-talk designed to buy time, undermine US pressure
item.description: President Donald Trump is sending both carrots and sticks towards Iran, stepping up military assets in the Middle East while also signaling talks are still on the table.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newsom to world: California open for business
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) directed his state to pursue “strategic partnerships” with foreign trading partners and is calling for California-made products to be exempted from the retaliatory tariffs targeting the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Houthis shoot down 3rd US reaper drone as Trump administration continues daily airstrikes
item.description: The Houthis shot down another U.S. Reaper drone, the third one that has been destroyed by the terror organization since early March.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kinzinger posts 'Step Brothers' clip to mock Trump tariffs
item.description: Former Rep. Adam Kinzinger (R-Ill.) posted a clip from the popular comedy "Step Brothers" to mock President Trump, Republicans and Fox News over the latest tariffs on foreign nations that were announced this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: In warning to Turkey, Israel hits strategic targets in Syria amid rising tensions
item.description: Israel targets Syrian airbases to warn Turkey against interference, as Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan faces protests at home and ramps up efforts to expand influence in Syria and region as a whole.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump calls for Marine Le Pen to be freed
item.description: President Trump on Thursday defended embattled French far-right leader Marine Le Pen, arguing the “witch hunt” against her is yet another instance of critics in Europe using “lawfare” to quash free speech.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 17 in Gaza as ground troops enter Palestinian territory's north
item.description: Israeli strikes have killed more than a dozen people in the Gaza Strip as Israel sends more ground troops into the Palestinian territory to ramp up its offensive against Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sri Lanka's target to be free of land mines by 2028 is under threat as US reviews aid
item.description: MANKULAM, Sri Lanka (AP) — Thavarathnam Pushparani fought on the front lines for the now-defeated Tamil Tiger rebels against the Sri Lankan forces in its decadeslong separatist war and later took to clearing the land mines on the same battle lines.
item.dc:creator: ERANGA JAYAWARDENA and KRISHAN FRANCIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strike on a school in Gaza kills at least 27 people, Palestinian health officials say
item.description: DEIR AL BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes killed at least 100 Palestinians across the Gaza Strip on Thursday, including 27 or more sheltering at a school, according to Palestinian medical authorities, in a stepped-up offensive that Israel’s military said is intended to pressure Hamas and e
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Yoon Suk Yeol had a rapid rise in South Korean politics before an abrupt downfall
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Yoon Suk Yeol’s political rise was fast: the former star prosecutor clinched South Korea’s presidency only a year after he entered politics.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yoon Suk Yeol removed as South Korea's president over short-lived martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s Constitutional Court unanimously removed Yoon Suk Yeol from office Friday, ending his tumultuous presidency and setting up a new election, four months after he threw the nation into turmoil with an ill-fated declaration of martial law.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A week after catastrophic earthquake, focus turns to a growing humanitarian crisis in Myanmar
item.description: Search teams have pulled more bodies from the ruins of buildings a week after a massive earthquake rocked Myanmar killing more than 3,300 people.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In Tunisia, snails inch toward replacing red meat as people turn to cheaper protein
item.description: AKOUDA, Tunisia (AP) — In fields outside their hometown in central Tunisia, an increasing number of unemployed young men are seeking a new way to make a living, picking snails off of rocks and leaves and collecting them in large plastic bags to take to the local market to be sold.
item.dc:creator: MEHDI EL AREM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump sued over China tariffs
item.description: President Trump was sued Thursday over the 20 percent tariffs he imposed on Chinese goods in the weeks leading up to Wednesday’s broader announcement. It marks the first known legal challenge against Trump’s tariffs, which have fulfilled a campaign promise and rattled financial markets.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: What to know about the Trump tariffs upending global trade and markets
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The trade war President Donald Trump promised has begun, threatening the world economy and straining the United States’ longstanding alliances in Europe and Asia.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump urged to review UN immunity, lax visa rules amid national security concerns
item.description: As the U.S. weighs U.N. funding cuts, experts urge the Trump administration to review the world body's visa process and staff immunity over continued security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Slovaks rally to protest against legislation that critics say is harmful to NGOs
item.description: BRATISLAVA, Slovakia (AP) — Thousands marched through Slovakia's capital on Thursday before a planned rally in front of parliament to condemn draft legislation that critics consider detrimental for many nongovernmental organizations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World leaders react to Trump’s sweeping tariffs: ‘tumultuous times’ ahead
item.description: World leaders had varied reactions to President Donald Trump's tariff announcements, with many pledging counter measures, while others hope to negotiate a removal of the import taxes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More than a dozen migrants die as boats sink off Greece and Turkey
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Two boats carrying migrants sank in the early hours of Thursday in the narrow stretch of sea between Turkey and a nearby Greek island, leaving at least 16 people dead, one missing and more than 40 survivors, Greek and Turkish officials said.
item.dc:creator: ELENA BECATOROS and ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Burma earthquake death toll rises to 3,085, humanitarian crisis deepens
item.description: More than 3,000 people have died from the 7.7 magnitude earthquake that struck Burma last week, which has sent the country into a deeper humanitarian crisis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hungary says it is withdrawing from International Criminal Court as Israeli PM Netanyahu visits country
item.description: Hungary announced it is withdrawing from the International Criminal Court as it is hosting Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu, who is wanted by the court for alleged war crimes in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel will establish a new security corridor across Gaza to pressure Hamas
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Wednesday that Israel is establishing a new security corridor across the Gaza Strip to pressure Hamas, suggesting it would cut off the southern city of Rafah, which Israel has ordered evacuated, from the rest of the Palestinian
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and ISAAC SCHARF, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Iran mulls preemptive strike on US base after Trump bomb threats
item.description: Iran is considering its options in deterring President Trump amid his repeated threats of direct military engagement by possibly launching its own strike on a U.S. base in the Indian Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Earthquake compounds Myanmar's humanitarian crisis as the death toll passes 2,000
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll in last week's massive earthquake in Myanmar has passed 2,000, state media said Monday, as accounts of some people's last moments emerged: Two hundred Buddhist monks crushed by a collapsing monastery. Fifty children killed when a preschool classroom crumbled.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation
